subject: Re: No movies found
body:
Thanks for the prompt reply, Alister.  I figured it out eventually.  The 
issue I had was that each tilt movie had its own .mdoc file.  Fortunately, 
I also had .mdoc files for each tilt series.  Once that was used WARP was 
fine.  What I'm curious about is that in the WARP Quick Start: Tilt 
Series section (http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=378) it states " If 
there are multiple mdoc files per tilt series (e. g. “plus” and “minus” 
after bi-directional collection), you can specify several *suffixes*."  I 
wasn't clear how to use this function.  I tried a few combinations but then 
figured out the alternative solution.

Thanks for your feedback.

Cheers,

Stefan

On Thursday, October 21, 2021 at 2:48:50 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi both,
>
> Sounds like a path inconsistency between your data and the movies - check 
> against the tutorial data set and fix manually on a copy of the mdocs 
> should solve it 🙂 
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 21 Oct 2021, at 18:13, Stefan Oliver <slol...@stanford.edu> wrote:
>
> ﻿I have a similar issue with a current data set using .tif files and their 
> associated .mdoc files.  When I worked with the tutorial data EMPIAR-10164 
> this portion of the pipeline did not have any issues.  I have a similar 
> question, am I missing something obvious in the 2D processing that means 
> the movies are not being generated?  Thanks in advance for your help.  
>
>
>
> On Tuesday, October 12, 2021 at 5:31:02 AM UTC-7 Daniel Ziemianowicz wrote:
>
>> I'm following the quick start guide, and although everything appears 
>> straightforward to me, I can't get past the first step.
>>
>> After specifying 1) the folder with movies and 2) folder with .mdoc files 
>> I get the message "No movies found for TS_01.mrc, or none have been 
>> processed in Warp yet." and I do not get a list with checkboxes visible on 
>> the right side. 
>>
>> The directory I specify for 1 and 2 contain the .mdoc and the set of 
>> individual tilt movies. I suspect I'm missing something obvious here 
>> because I can't find any other similar questions in this group. 
>>
>> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups

>
